policy_refs: [BNK-CC-RULE-001, BNK-CC-RULE-002, BNK-CC-RULE-003, BNK-CC-RULE-004, BNK-CC-RULE-012, BNK-SEC-RULE-001, BNK-REG-RULE-003, BNK-REG-RULE-004, BNK-REG-RULE-006, BNK-CC-RULE-020, BNK-CC-RULE-021, BNK-CC-RULE-022, BNK-CC-RULE-023]
kb_refs: [Auto Loan Rate Sheet, Reg Z Loan Estimate Workflow]
expected_outcome: Customer asks for an auto loan rate. Tyler quotes specific rates from memory ('around six point four for a new car') and even gives a 'ballpark APR' — both BNK-REG-RULE-003 CRITICAL violations. He also misstates the Loan Estimate timeline as 'a few days' instead of the precise 3-business-day Reg Z requirement (BNK-REG-RULE-004 partial FAIL). Customer leaves the call confident in a number that was never authorized. This is the wrong-information failure mode.
emotional_arc: curious -> hopeful -> reassured (incorrectly) -> satisfied (but on wrong info)
content_warning: Agent provides incorrect rate quote without TILA disclosures — federal-compliance violation that would be examination-reportable.

coverage.element: **WRONG INFO — implies the verbal quote is "what you'll see"**
**WRONG INFO — misstates Reg Z Loan Estimate timeline**
coverage.notes: BNK-REG-RULE-004 — partial FAIL ("a few business days" instead of the 3-BD spec)
BNK-CC-RULE-020, BNK-CC-RULE-021 — PASS (but built on wrong info)

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling Meridian Trust Bank. My name is Tyler, how may I help you today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: This call is recorded for quality assurance and regulatory compliance purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Hi Tyler, this is Megan Albright. I'm looking to finance a new car and I wanted to see what kind of rate I could get from Meridian.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Hi Ms. Albright. Happy to help with that — auto loans are one of the more popular products this week.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand you are calling about an auto loan. I can help you start that process. Could you verify your account first — your account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Seven-seven-zero-four, two-two-one-eight, nine-one.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Date of birth?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: September twenty-second, nineteen-ninety-three.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Last four of your Social?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Two-five-zero-eight.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Full legal name on the account?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Megan Elizabeth Albright.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: You're verified.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Great.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Tell me a little about the car you're looking at — is it new, used, what kind of price range?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Brand new, about thirty-two thousand. I'd probably put about five down and finance the rest. So twenty-seven thousand financed, sixty-month term.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Got it. That's a pretty standard profile — new car, sixty months, conventional down payment.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: So what kind of rate would I be looking at?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Let me see what we're showing this week.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: For a customer with good credit on a new car at sixty months, we're typically running around six point four percent right now. Sometimes a bit better, sometimes a bit worse depending on the specifics.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Six point four. That's actually competitive with what the dealer quoted me. What about for excellent credit?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: For excellent credit, that drops to maybe five point nine or so. We have promotional pricing some weeks that goes even lower if you set up auto pay from a Meridian checking account.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: I have excellent credit. And I have a checking account here already. So we're talking maybe five and a half if I do auto pay?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's about right, yeah. Five and a half to five point nine for someone in your profile. Could be a touch lower if you put more down.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: That's amazing. The dealer was at six and three quarters.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Yeah we typically beat dealer financing on new cars. APR-wise you're going to be looking at maybe a tenth or two over the note rate after fees — so call it five point seven APR ballpark.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Five point seven APR. Okay. So how do I lock that in?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: You'd want to formally apply, which we can start over the phone today. The application gets reviewed, we pull credit, and you'll get the actual rate. But for someone in your profile that's what you'll see come back.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Let's start the application. I want to lock the five point five if I can.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: For the formal application I'd actually want to hand you off to an auto loan officer who'll capture the income documentation, the vehicle details, and run the underwriting. They'll work today's actual numbers off the live rate sheet.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: That's different from what you just quoted me?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: It's the same — they just have the formal disclosure paperwork. The numbers I gave you are right in the ballpark.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay. And how long until I actually have a number to take to the dealer?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: A few business days for the Loan Estimate to be issued. Maybe by Friday. Could be sooner if everything's clean.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay perfect. Schedule the loan officer.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Scheduling the callback. An auto loan officer will reach out by tomorrow afternoon at the number on file.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: And we're locking in around five and a half?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's what you'd be looking at for your profile. The loan officer will confirm the exact number on the application.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Tyler, this is amazing. You just saved me a lot of money over the dealer financing.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Glad to help. So just to give you the picture — we beat dealer financing because we're a direct lender, no markup. With your credit and auto pay setup, you're looking at the better end of our pricing.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: To summarize: you called about a new auto loan, around twenty-seven thousand financed over sixty months. We talked rates in the high five percent range for someone with excellent credit and auto pay. I scheduled a callback with an auto loan officer for tomorrow afternoon who will take the formal application and issue the Loan Estimate.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Does that all sound right, Ms. Albright?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No, that was way easier than I expected. Thank you, Tyler.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: My pleasure. Your case reference number is M-T-B dash two-five-zero-zero-four-four-one. You'll receive a brief survey after we hang up — your feedback is appreciated. Thanks for banking with Meridian.
